body.title: Builder-wide: unify device-close + emit real proof at branch-merge/validation time
body.description:
**Problem:** A branch-builder (Aider, DickSimnel) structurally cannot emit a HEAD-valid proof_store artifact at build time — the impl lives on an unmerged branch in a throwaway clone, not at UU HEAD. Today BOTH builders close via a plain `cc_queue close` that the proof-on-close gate refuses (DickSimnel device.py:285; AiderDevice now closes shipped-unproven as the honest stopgap). Consequence (from the ticket's own premise): 'unvalidated builder throughput just shifts the queue to CC' — if every builder close is shipped-unproven and CC must eyeball each branch, that's validate-every-one, not Q4 spot-check-a-sample. The builder only reduces CC load once a real proof emits when the branch merges/validates.

**Proposed shape:** (1) Extract ONE shared device-close mechanism both builders call (homogeneity — one mechanism parameterized by addressee, not an aider snowflake vs a DS snowflake). (2) Emit the proof_store artifact at branch-merge/validation time (the merger/CC path): when the aider/DS branch is validated and merged to HEAD, run proof_emitter red->green against the ticket's test so the ticket flips from shipped-unproven to proven. Design the merge/validation trigger.

**Affected files:** new shared builder-close helper (e.g. unseen_university/devices/_builder_close.py or a capabilities mixin); unseen_university/devices/aider/device.py; unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/device.py; the merge/validation path (locate).
**Design rules:** homogeneity over special-case; proof-on-close (CP1); no SQLite; log the close/merge crossings.
**Scope boundary:** shared close + merge-time proof emission + wiring both builders. NOT: the aider real-UU-ticket proof (T-aider-real-uu-ticket-proof).
**Test plan:** red->green on the shared close helper (gate-pass -> shipped-unproven close args; merge -> proof emitted -> ticket proven). 
**Completion criteria:** both builders use one close mechanism; a merged builder branch emits a HEAD-valid proof and flips the ticket to proven.
**intention:** I intend that a builder's throughput reduces CC's queue instead of shifting eyeball-validation onto CC, by making the proof real at merge time.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Shared close + merge-time proof, both provable. (1) BuilderCloseMixin (unseen_university/devices/_builder_close.py) — ONE branch-builder close both AiderDevice+DickSimnelDevice inherit (homogeneity); fixes DickSimnel's gate-refused plain close. (2) builder_merge_proof.emit_merge_proof — after CC merges a builder branch (parent_ref=pre-merge), emits a HEAD-valid red->green proof so the close-gate flips shipped-unproven->proven; trigger is CC's merge step (no merger device, per scope). Proven end-to-end via a REAL local git merge (red 1ac42077->green, proof bound). Mixin unit-guarded; both builders + dicksimnel suite green.
